q:
The bell is not ringing.
When are you allowed to overtake a vehicle on this level crossing?

A. Only if the barrier is open AND the white lunar light is lit at the same time.
B. If the barrier is open and the white lunar light is not lit, because it is defective.
e:
LESSON 22
You may overtake a vehicle on the left at a level crossing when the barrier is fully open and the white lunar light is shining at the same time.
But if the bell is not ringing, the barrier is open, but the lights are defective, you may also overtake a vehicle on the left.

clean_e:
LESSON 22
You may overtake a vehicle on the left at a level crossing when the barrier is fully open and the white lunar light is shining at the same time.
But if the bell is not ringing, the barrier is open, but the lights are defective, you may also overtake a vehicle on the left.

q:
You approach this bus stop by car and you see that the bus has its hazard warning lights on.
Do you have to slow down and give way?

A. Yes, only outside built-up areas.
B. Yes, only in built-up areas.
C. No.
e:
LESSON 22
You must give way when a bus wants to leave a bus stop in a built-up area.
In that case, the left indicator must be flashing, not the hazard warning lights.
When the hazard warning lights are on, this simply means that the bus is stopped to allow passengers to get on or off.
clean_q:
You approach this bus stop by car and you see that the bus has its hazard warning lights on.
Do you have to slow down and give way?
A. Yes, only outside built-up areas.
B. Yes, only in built-up areas.
C. No.
clean_e:
LESSON  22  
You must give way when a bus wants to leave a bus stop in a built-up area.
In that case, the left indicator must be flashing, not the hazard warning lights.
When the hazard warning lights are on, this simply means that the bus is stopped to allow passengers to get on or off.

q:
Fill in a number:
The police allow you to drive on a special reserved route.
Which traffic signal has the same meaning as a green light?
e:
LESSON 18 + 22
On a special overrun lane, the signals mean:
1️⃣ An inverted triangle → same meaning as a green light.
2️⃣ A circle → same meaning as a steady amber light.
3️⃣ A horizontal bar → same meaning as a red light.
4️⃣ A diagonal bar to the left → permission to drive only in that direction.

LESSON 18 + 22

2️⃣ A circle → same meaning as a steady amber light.
3️⃣ A horizontal bar → same meaning as a red light.
4️⃣ A diagonal bar to the left → permission to drive only in that direction.

B. No.

⚠️ This rule does not apply to:
• coaches,
• buses used for school transport or employee transport.
